title: CLI: keyfiles created without 0600 permissions
description: cli/src/commands.rs:117 and :3953 create identity.key and wallet.key with std::fs::write(), which respects the process umask. With a typical umask of 0022, the files are created 0644 — readable by all users on the system. The encrypted age ciphertext is exposed, enabling offline passphrase attacks. Fix: create with a restrictive mode before writing, e.g. use std::fs::OpenOptions + set_permissions(0o600) on Unix, or the nix crate's O_CREAT with mode 0600.
